seoTitle: Not sure yet — show everything after 10th — courses, exams & careers | SkillKite
seoDescription: After 10th — every stream and option compared, if you're still deciding

guide.heading: Your options after 10th — SkillKite
guide.greeting: Hi there! You told us you are not yet sure about your interest area and want to both study and earn. That is completely okay — many students feel the same way at this stage. The most relevant options for you are listed first — read through all of them before deciding.

sections.title: Study options
sections.intro: Since you are still exploring, we have listed options that keep the most doors open first. Read all options before deciding — do not rush this choice.

options.name: 12th Science with Maths + Biology (PCMB)
options.whatIsIt: You study Physics, Chemistry, Maths, AND Biology together in 11th and 12th. This is the broadest science stream available and keeps both engineering and medical paths open at the same time.
options.whoFor: Students who are curious about science but have not yet decided between medical and engineering. Be aware — this is a heavy workload with five subjects, so you need to be genuinely hardworking.
options.leadsTo: Engineering colleges (JEE), medical colleges (NEET), pharmacy, agriculture, BSc, and more. Maximum flexibility of any stream.
options.keyExams: JEE Main, JEE Advanced, NEET-UG (all highly competitive — consistent preparation from Day 1 is essential)
options.timeCommitment: 2 years (11th + 12th), then competitive exam preparation on top

options.name: Polytechnic Diploma (3 years, direct after 10th)
options.whatIsIt: A government-recognised 3-year technical diploma in fields like Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Computer Science, or Electronics. You can join directly after 10th without waiting for 12th.
options.whoFor: Students who want practical, hands-on learning and want to start earning sooner. Admission difficulty varies by state and by branch — popular branches in good colleges can be competitive.
options.leadsTo: Government and private sector jobs as a Junior Engineer or Technician, or lateral entry into 2nd year of a BE/BTech degree later. Good earning potential without a full 4-year degree.
options.keyExams: State-level Polytechnic Entrance Exams (varies by state — examples: JEECUP in UP, DET in Maharashtra, AP POLYCET in Andhra Pradesh)

options.name: 12th Commerce
options.whatIsIt: You study Accountancy, Business Studies, Economics, and optionally Maths or Informatics Practices. This stream covers how businesses and money work.
options.whoFor: Students who are interested in business, finance, banking, or entrepreneurship, or who want a slightly less science-heavy but still career-focused path.
options.leadsTo: CA (Chartered Accountant), BBA, BCom, MBA, Banking jobs, Stock Market, or starting your own business. CA is very respected and earns very well but requires long dedication.
options.keyExams: CA Foundation (after 12th), CUET for central university admissions, various state university entrance exams
options.timeCommitment: 2 years (11th + 12th), then degree or professional course

options.name: 12th Arts / Humanities
options.whatIsIt: You study subjects like History, Geography, Political Science, Sociology, Psychology, or Economics. This stream is often misunderstood — it actually opens a very wide range of careers.
options.whoFor: Students who enjoy reading, writing, thinking about society, or who want to prepare for UPSC Civil Services and government exams. Also great if you are interested in law, journalism, or teaching.
options.leadsTo: UPSC IAS/IPS, Law (CLAT), Journalism, Social Work, Psychology, Teaching, Mass Communication, Design, and many more fields. Government exam preparation is very strong from this stream.
options.keyExams: CLAT (for law), CUET, NID/NIFT (for design), no mandatory entrance for many BA admissions

options.name: 12th Science with Maths (PCM)
options.whatIsIt: You study Physics, Chemistry, and Maths in 11th and 12th. This is the standard path for students aiming at engineering, architecture, or computer science.
options.whoFor: Students who enjoy problem-solving, numbers, and technology. JEE is extremely competitive — only choose this if you are genuinely interested in Maths and are ready to put in serious effort.
options.leadsTo: Engineering (BTech/BE), Architecture (B.Arch), BSc Maths/Physics/Computer Science, Data Science, and many government technical jobs.
options.keyExams: JEE Main, JEE Advanced (very competitive), BITSAT, MHT-CET, state-level engineering entrance exams

options.name: 12th Science with Biology (PCB)
options.whatIsIt: You study Physics, Chemistry, and Biology in 11th and 12th. This is the standard path for students aiming at medicine, nursing, pharmacy, or life sciences.
options.whoFor: Students who care about health, helping people, or living organisms. NEET is one of India's most competitive exams — be honest with yourself about the level of preparation it requires.
options.leadsTo: MBBS, BDS, BAMS, BHMS, BSc Nursing, B.Pharmacy, B.Sc Agriculture, Physiotherapy, Biotechnology.
options.keyExams: NEET-UG (very competitive — lakhs of students appear each year for limited seats), AIIMS is now under NEET

options.name: Paramedical Diploma after 10th (DMLT, ANM, X-Ray Tech, etc.)
options.whatIsIt: Short diploma courses in healthcare support roles such as Lab Technology (DMLT), Nursing Assistant (ANM), X-Ray Technician, OT Technician, or Dental Assistant. These are available directly after 10th.
options.whoFor: Students interested in the health sector who want a job faster without spending years in full MBBS preparation. Honest note — salaries start modest but grow with experience.
options.leadsTo: Jobs in hospitals, diagnostic labs, clinics, and government health centres. Some diplomas also allow you to pursue further degrees like BSc MLT later.
options.keyExams: Admission is mostly merit-based or state-level entrance (varies by institution and state). Government paramedical colleges are more competitive than private ones.
options.timeCommitment: 1 to 2 years depending on the course

sections.title: Job & earning options
sections.intro: Since you want to earn while you study or explore, here are realistic ways a 10th-pass student can start building income or a skill. None of these require a degree to begin — but all of them require genuine effort and consistency.

options.name: Graphic Design (Canva / Figma)
options.whatIsIt: Learn to create posters, logos, social media posts, and presentations using free tools like Canva or the industry-standard Figma. Thousands of free tutorials are available on YouTube.
options.whoFor: Students who enjoy visual creativity and are comfortable spending time on a phone or laptop. This skill is in very high demand from small businesses and content creators.
options.leadsTo: Freelance projects, part-time work for local businesses, internships, and eventually a full career in UI/UX Design or Brand Design.
options.timeCommitment: 2 to 4 months of self-learning to reach a beginner freelance level

options.name: Content Creation (YouTube / Instagram Reels)
options.whatIsIt: Create videos, reels, or posts around a topic you enjoy — education, comedy, cooking, travel, local news, or anything else. Consistency and quality matter more than expensive equipment.
options.whoFor: Students who are comfortable in front of a camera or behind one, and who can commit to posting regularly. Income takes time — do not expect quick money.
options.leadsTo: Ad revenue, brand sponsorships, affiliate marketing, or using your audience to sell your own products or services.
options.timeCommitment: Ongoing — typically 6 to 12 months before meaningful income begins

options.name: Mobile Phone Repair
options.whatIsIt: Learn to diagnose and fix common smartphone problems — broken screens, battery replacements, software issues. Short courses are available at local ITI centres or on YouTube.
options.whoFor: Students who are curious about how gadgets work and enjoy hands-on technical tasks. Very high demand in every city and town in India.
options.leadsTo: Your own small repair shop, employment at a mobile service centre, or expanding into selling accessories and second-hand phones.
options.timeCommitment: 1 to 3 months of training to handle basic repairs

options.name: Meesho / Reselling
options.whatIsIt: Resell products through apps like Meesho or Glowroad by sharing product links on WhatsApp and social media. You earn a margin on every sale without holding any inventory.
options.whoFor: Students with a good social network and basic smartphone skills. Low risk to start — no upfront investment needed. Income is variable and not guaranteed.
options.leadsTo: Pocket money to start with, and over time can grow into a larger e-commerce or distribution business if you build it seriously.
options.timeCommitment: Can start within a week; serious income needs consistent effort over months

options.name: Data Entry / Typing Jobs
options.whatIsIt: Earn money by doing data entry, form filling, or copy-typing work for companies or freelance clients. Requires a laptop or access to a computer and a decent typing speed.
options.whoFor: Students who are patient, accurate, and have access to a computer. Be careful — many fake data entry job offers exist online. Stick to verified platforms like Upwork or local office contacts.
options.leadsTo: Part-time income while studying, and the typing and computer skills gained help in almost every future job or government exam.
options.timeCommitment: A few weeks to build typing speed; work can start quickly after that

options.name: Tally / Basic Accounting
options.whatIsIt: Learn Tally software and basic bookkeeping. Tally is used by almost every small business in India to manage accounts and file GST returns.
options.whoFor: Students who are comfortable with numbers and are considering Commerce or want a practical skill that pays quickly. Short courses are available at local computer institutes.
options.leadsTo: Part-time or full-time work as an accounts assistant in local shops, firms, or offices. Good stepping stone toward a career in finance or CA.
options.timeCommitment: 1 to 3 months for a basic course

options.name: Photography / Videography
options.whatIsIt: Learn to shoot and edit photos or videos for events, local businesses, or social media clients. You can start with a decent smartphone camera and free editing apps.
options.whoFor: Students with a creative eye who enjoy storytelling through visuals. Takes time to build a client base but can become a strong income source.
options.leadsTo: Freelance wedding and event photography, product photography for businesses, YouTube or Instagram content production, or eventually a full creative studio.
options.timeCommitment: 2 to 4 months to learn basics; building clients takes longer

options.name: Tailoring / Stitching
options.whatIsIt: Learn to stitch and alter clothing — from basic repairs to making full garments. Formal courses are available at government-run institutes like the National Institute of Fashion Technology or local tailoring centres.
options.whoFor: Students who enjoy working with their hands and have an interest in fashion or clothing. Particularly strong earning potential in smaller towns where good tailors are in high demand.
options.leadsTo: Home-based tailoring business, boutique work, costume design, or eventually your own fashion brand.
options.timeCommitment: 3 to 6 months for a beginner certificate course

guide.closingMessage: Save this guide and discuss it with your parents or teachers. When you finish 12th, come back to SkillKite — we'll build you a detailed career roadmap. Share it with a friend who needs it too. 🪁
